[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row css=”.vc_custom_1636434630693{margin-right: -5% !important;margin-left: -5% !important;background-color: #ffffff !important;}”][vc_column][vc_row_inner css=”.vc_custom_1748250928711{margin-right: 5% !important;margin-left: 5% !important;}”][vc_column_inner][vc_single_image image=”56084″ img_size=”full” alignment=”center”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row css=”.vc_custom_1636522597176{margin-right: -5% !important;margin-left: -5% !important;background-color: #ffffff !important;}”][vc_column][vc_row_inner content_placement=”middle” css=”.vc_custom_1636625660188{margin-right: 5% !important;margin-left: 5% !important;background-color: #f0f2f2 !important;}”][vc_column_inner width=”1/2″ css=”.vc_custom_1636524634550{margin-top: -10px !important;background-color: #ffffff !important;}”][vc_single_image image=”56088″ img_size=”full”][/vc_column_inner][vc_column_inner width=”1/2″][vc_column_text css=”.vc_custom_1749697308916{margin-right: 20px !important;margin-left: 20px !important;padding-right: 5px !important;padding-left: 5px !important;}”]Elitewheels is a big proponent of marginal gains and knows that many small improvements can add up. The Velo Six II features a 21mm internal rim width improving rolling resistance when compatible with wider tires, which have the added benefit of improved comfort on less than optimal road surfaces as well as increasing grip in wet conditions.

To push performance even further, the rim’s cross-sectional profile has been newly redesigned to significantly boost lateral stiffness. Advanced carbon fiber layering techniques enhance rigidity without adding weight, even with a larger cross-section. In addition, the use of complex surface curvatures and refined contouring dramatically improves overall stiffness. The Velo Six II now delivers greater impact resistance, making it not only faster but tougher than ever.

The Velo Six II features Glossy Raw Finish which is created by putting a clearcoat over the rim without additional finish. This allows for you to see the actual UD carbon layup. Any small imperfections that are covered up by traditional Matte, Glossy, 3K, or 12k finish, are made visible. These cosmetic imperfections exist on all carbon wheels regardless of the finish and have no impact on performance or durability.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row css=”.vc_custom_1636522597176{margin-right: -5% !important;margin-left: -5% !important;background-color: #ffffff !important;}”][vc_column][vc_row_inner content_placement=”middle” css=”.vc_custom_1636625660188{margin-right: 5% !important;margin-left: 5% !important;background-color: #f0f2f2 !important;}”][vc_column_inner width=”1/2″ css=”.vc_custom_1636524634550{margin-top: -10px !important;background-color: #ffffff !important;}”][vc_single_image image=”56086″ img_size=”full”][/vc_column_inner][vc_column_inner width=”1/2″][vc_column_text css=”.vc_custom_1747725284361{margin-right: 20px !important;margin-left: 20px !important;padding-right: 5px !important;padding-left: 5px !important;}”]

SPOKES

What separates this wheelset from the pack is the shape of the spokes. While there are other one piece road wheels available, not all of the spokes are designed with aerodynamics in mind. Our engineers have optimized the wheelset to minimize the coefficient of drag through by reducing the spokes’ frontal area and mimicking the ideal teardrop shape.

The connection between the spokes and the rim has also been refined, ensuring a smoother structural transition. These mechanical improvements result in more efficient and seamless power transfer across the wheelset.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row css=”.vc_custom_1636436261896{margin-right: -5% !important;margin-left: -5% !important;background-color: #ffffff !important;}”][vc_column][vc_row_inner content_placement=”middle” css=”.vc_custom_1636625761971{margin-right: 5% !important;margin-left: 5% !important;background-color: #f0f2f2 !important;}”][vc_column_inner width=”1/2″][vc_single_image image=”56087″ img_size=”full”][/vc_column_inner][vc_column_inner width=”1/2″][vc_column_text css=”.vc_custom_1747907486021{margin-right: 20px !important;margin-left: 20px !important;padding-right: 5px !important;padding-left: 5px !important;}”]

PERFORMANCE HUB

Elitewheels designs and manufactures its own hubs. The front and rear hubs are made of 7075 aluminum which is known for it’s high strength.

Through advanced CAE (Computer-Aided Engineering) analysis, the unique structural characteristics of the rim were identified. This led to a comprehensive hub redesign to better align with the system’s load distribution.

The hub features a 50T ratchet system with a 7.2°engagement angle for quicker response. Unlike traditional flat ratchets, the specially angled tooth surfaces allow automatic alignment, increasing engagement area and improving both stability and reliability. Additionally, the tapered design reduces stress on the ratchet’s tooth surface, minimizing wear and extending the hub’s service life.

Discover the Hub Sound Here[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row css=”.vc_custom_1636436261896{margin-right: -5% !important;margin-left: -5% !important;background-color: #ffffff !important;}”][vc_column][vc_row_inner content_placement=”middle” css=”.vc_custom_1636625761971{margin-right: 5% !important;margin-left: 5% !important;background-color: #f0f2f2 !important;}”][vc_column_inner width=”1/2″][vc_single_image image=”56494″ img_size=”full” alignment=”center”][/vc_column_inner][vc_column_inner width=”1/2″][vc_column_text css=”.vc_custom_1747731700555{margin-right: 20px !important;margin-left: 20px !important;padding-right: 5px !important;padding-left: 5px !important;}”]

CERAMIC BEARINGS

The S&S ceramic bearings in the Velo Six II were specifically chosen for their low rolling resistance and excellent durability. Using Fanuc CNC machines, our hubs are machined to a tolerance of 0.02mm. This precision is especially important for ceramic bearings to minimize rolling resistance and increase longevity.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][/vc_column][vc_column][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner width=”1/6″][/vc_column_inner][vc_column_inner width=”2/3″][vc_column_text]

In order to provide riders with the best wheelsets, we seek to update and improve them as technology advances. Elitewheels reserves the right to change, add or remove the information contained on this website at any time, including equipment, specifications, models, colors, materials, and pricing, without prior notice. The shipped products will be based on the actual order information. Elitewheels thanks you for your understanding and support.

Compared to our previous TT wheels, the new Drive TT features a wider 22mm internal rim width without compromising aerodynamics. This upgrade allows for the use of 700×25–40c tires, improving ride comfort over long distances and rough surfaces.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row css=”.vc_custom_1636522597176{margin-right: -5% !important;margin-left: -5% !important;background-color: #ffffff !important;}”][vc_column][vc_row_inner content_placement=”middle” css=”.vc_custom_1636625660188{margin-right: 5% !important;margin-left: 5% !important;background-color: #f0f2f2 !important;}”][vc_column_inner width=”1/2″ css=”.vc_custom_1636524634550{margin-top: -10px !important;background-color: #ffffff !important;}”][vc_single_image image=”57045″ img_size=”full”][/vc_column_inner][vc_column_inner width=”1/2″][vc_column_text css=”.vc_custom_1749780605822{margin-right: 20px !important;margin-left: 20px !important;padding-right: 5px !important;padding-left: 5px !important;}”]Marginal Gains

At Elitewheels, we believe in the power of marginal gains—small, deliberate improvements that add up to a big performance edge. That philosophy is built into every detail of the new Drive TT wheel.

For the first time, we’ve introduced the sail effect design concept, refined through extensive data analysis to enhance aerodynamic efficiency. (Sail effect: At certain yaw angles, the interaction between the wheel and wind direction can generate forward thrust, reducing aerodynamic drag and lowering rider power output.) This was validated through wind tunnel testing at Silverstone, UK, where under optimal conditions, the Drive TT required as little as 76W of power at 45 km/h -20°yaw angle.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row css=”.vc_custom_1636436261896{margin-right: -5% !important;margin-left: -5% !important;background-color: #ffffff !important;}”][vc_column][vc_row_inner content_placement=”middle” css=”.vc_custom_1636625761971{margin-right: 5% !important;margin-left: 5% !important;background-color: #f0f2f2 !important;}”][vc_column_inner width=”1/2″][vc_single_image image=”57038″ img_size=”full”][/vc_column_inner][vc_column_inner width=”1/2″][vc_column_text css=”.vc_custom_1750325386038{margin-right: 20px !important;margin-left: 20px !important;padding-right: 5px !important;padding-left: 5px !important;}”]Performance Hub

Crafted from high-strength 7075 aluminum, both the front and rear hubs are built for durability and performance. They are equipped with premium S&S Ceramic for smooth, long-lasting operation.

The lightweight freehub features an upgraded 50T tapered ratchet system with a 7.2° engagement angle, delivering rapid responsiveness. Unlike traditional flat ratchets, the specially angled tooth surfaces enable automatic self-alignment, which increases the engagement area. This design not only enhances stability and reliability but also reduces stress on the tooth surfaces, minimizing wear and extending the hub’s overall lifespan.

The Drive Six II combines striking aerodynamics with cutting-edge engineering. Its one-piece six-spoke carbon design maximizes stiffness and efficiency, delivering sharper cornering, faster acceleration, and power transfer beyond what steel-spoked wheels can achieve. Silver decals and a distinctive marbled finish make it as visually unique as it is performance-driven.

Through advanced CAE analysis and an optimized carbon layup, the Drive Six II weighs just 1450g per pair 70g lighter than its predecessor achieving the ideal balance of stiffness and lightweight performance.

Compared to the first generation, the Drive Six II features an enlarged cross-section with a refined 1:1.2 aspect ratio, promoting more uniform force distribution and reduced stress concentration. The structural upgrade delivers a 25% increase in forward rigidity at the spoke interfaces, significantly improving power transmission efficiency. In addition, impact resistance has been enhanced by 15% at these critical areas, ensuring outstanding durability and reliability even on the most demanding terrain.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row css=”.vc_custom_1636522597176{margin-right: -5% !important;margin-left: -5% !important;background-color: #ffffff !important;}”][vc_column][vc_row_inner content_placement=”middle” css=”.vc_custom_1636625660188{margin-right: 5% !important;margin-left: 5% !important;background-color: #f0f2f2 !important;}”][vc_column_inner width=”1/2″ css=”.vc_custom_1636524634550{margin-top: -10px !important;background-color: #ffffff !important;}”][vc_single_image image=”67603″ img_size=”full” onclick=”link_image” title=”Six s spoke Rim type”][/vc_column_inner][vc_column_inner width=”1/2″][vc_column_text css=”.vc_custom_1756801460613{margin-right: 20px !important;margin-left: 20px !important;padding-right: 5px !important;padding-left: 5px !important;}”]

RIM TYPE

The Drive Six II features a hookless, full-carbon rim, engineered from the ground up for modern gravel adventures. Designed for tubeless setups, it features a wider 30mm internal and 36mm external profile-optimized for 700×50c tires while maintaining full compatibility with a broad range of 700×45-89c options.

*In accordance with the recommendations and standards implemented by the ETRTO, tires fit on hookless rims should not have a pressure exceeding 73 PSI(5 bar). The Drive Six II is not compatible with tires requiring a minimum pressure higher than this value. Over-inflation could cause a blowout and cause potential injury.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row css=”.vc_custom_1636522597176{margin-right: -5% !important;margin-left: -5% !important;background-color: #ffffff !important;}”][vc_column][vc_row_inner content_placement=”middle” css=”.vc_custom_1636625660188{margin-right: 5% !important;margin-left: 5% !important;background-color: #f0f2f2 !important;}”][vc_column_inner width=”1/2″ css=”.vc_custom_1636524634550{margin-top: -10px !important;background-color: #ffffff !important;}”][vc_single_image image=”67602″ img_size=”full” onclick=”link_image” title=”drive six gravel hub”][/vc_column_inner][vc_column_inner width=”1/2″][vc_column_text css=”.vc_custom_1756801490143{margin-right: 20px !important;margin-left: 20px !important;padding-right: 5px !important;padding-left: 5px !important;}”]

HUBS

At the heart of the Drive Six II is our newly developed Kinetic hub, engineered for precision, durability, and efficiency. Crafted from forged and CNC-machined 7075 aluminum, it is both lightweight and exceptionally strong. The hub employs a 50T conical star ratchet system, offering a larger engagement surface compared to traditional pawl designs. This not only ensures quicker, more consistent engagement but also extends longevity under demanding conditions.

To further enhance performance, the Kinetic hub is fitted with a reinforced spring and a new waterproof seal, minimizing the chances of slipping, skipping, or water ingress. Inside, high-grade S&S ceramic bearings reduce rolling resistance and extend lifespan, while our 5-axis CNC machining guarantees perfect bearing seat tolerances of just 0.02mm. The result is a hub that saves valuable watts, maximizes efficiency, and delivers unmatched reliability on every ride.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][/vc_column][vc_column][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner width=”1/6″][/vc_column_inner][vc_column_inner width=”2/3″][vc_column_text]

At Elitewheels, every generation of innovation paves the way for the next. Introducing the Drive Six II engineered from the ground up to redefine what’s possible in carbon gravel wheels. Seamlessly blending striking aesthetics with relentless performance, this wheelset is built to captivate onlookers and conquer every surface with unwavering confidence.

Featuring a 30mm internal and 36mm external width, the Drive Six II weighs just 1450g per pair while delivering a 25% increase in forward rigidity and a 15% improvement in impact resistance at critical stress points.

The Drive Six II ensures a stiffer, faster, and more dependable ride, empowering gravel riders of every level to push further, faster, and with complete confidence.

*Instructions for the first assembly: Tubeless tires must be ridden for at least 25 km directly after the sealant is added so as to ensure they are air-tight.
